summary: nearest server is not selected when useUnifiedTopology is set
description:
Version 3.3.5 of mongodb driver does not select nearest mongodb server when readPreference is set to "nearest" and useUnifiedTopology is true.

Each query randomly chooses server from list of available servers regardless of its roundrip time. For example, my servers have roundtrip times of 10 ms, 160 ms and 190 ms and all of them gets selected randomly for query.

When useUnifiedTopology is set to false, everything works fine and nearest server with lowest latency is automatically selected.

summary: Malicious server can accept incomplete SCRAM authentication and bypass mutual authentication
description: See SECURITY-621

summary: tls option in connection URI has no effect
description: Connection URI should support tls=true option but the only option which works right now is ssl=true. When tls=true is passed, driver connects over unencrypted channel

summary: Default Port is not applied
Remove wasteful empty Buffer allocations in `Connection`
description: Our logic to read data off the wire does a _whole bunch_ of {{Buffer.alloc(0)}}, simply to indicate a "reset" of internal buffer state. This is wasteful, requiring many needless allocations when we could simply assign the value to {{null}}/{{undefined}}.

summary: Unordered bulk write reports wrong error indexes
Sporadic connection errors with AWS Lambda
description:
We are experiencing random connection errors in our app when using AWS Lambda. We are experiencing the issues both for Node 10.x and Node 12.x, and are using mongo driver version 3.4.0 with the following settings:
{code:java}
{ 
   useUnifiedTopology: true,
   bufferMaxEntries: 0
}
{code}
This caused the lambda functions to randomly time out with error code 502.

After changing *connectTimeoutMS* and *socketTimeoutMS* to *3000*, we got the following error message from the sporadically failing connections.
{code:java}
{
    "errorType": "TypeError",
    "errorMessage": "Cannot read property 'payload' of null",
    "stack": [
        "TypeError: Cannot read property 'payload' of null",
        "    at sendAuthCommand (/var/task/node_modules/mongodb/lib/core/auth/scram.js:244:47)",
        "    at _callback (/var/task/node_modules/mongodb/lib/core/connection/connect.js:320:5)",
        "    at Connection.errorHandler (/var/task/node_modules/mongodb/lib/core/connection/connect.js:336:5)",
        "    at Object.onceWrapper (events.js:286:20)",
        "    at Connection.emit (events.js:198:13)",
        "    at Connection.EventEmitter.emit (domain.js:448:20)",
        "    at TLSSocket.<anonymous> (/var/task/node_modules/mongodb/lib/core/connection/connection.js:353:10)",
        "    at Object.onceWrapper (events.js:286:20)",
        "    at TLSSocket.emit (events.js:198:13)",
        "    at TLSSocket.EventEmitter.emit (domain.js:448:20)"
    ]
}
{code}
We are caching our connection outside the lambda handler according to this blog post:

[https://www.mongodb.com/blog/post/optimizing-aws-lambda-performance-with-mongodb-atlas-and-nodejs]

 

Appreciate any help, Kind regards.

summary: Synchronous errors are swallowed by executeOperation
description: In some circumstances synchronously thrown errors are caught by {{executeOperation}} and reported through an optionally provided callback, or {{Promise}}. The logic of {{executeOperation}} can be dramatically simplified while also allowing synchronous errors to be thrown as expected.

summary: Use a dedicated monitoring thread
description: The driver presently uses a server's connection pool in order to do monitoring checks. This has well documented limitation, in particular in the face of a default socket timeout of unlimited. We should introduce a {{Monitor}} class, and a dedicated monitoring socket for each server in a {{Topology}}.

summary: Make server selection errors more informative
description: There are a lot of reasons a server selection timeout error might occur, and even though we include the {{reason}} in an attached field, most people only see {{Server selection timed out after 30000ms}}. Let's make this message more informative

summary: allow custom BSON library to be passed in
description: The {{AutoEncrypter}} class currently assumes it can get access to a {{BSON}} instances from the passed in client's topology, however, that topology might not always exist at the time the module is called. We should allow passing in a {{BSON}} instances directly through {{options}}

summary: bypassAutoEncryption should be a concern of the CSFLE module
description: Bypassing auto encryption is currently handled on the driver side, requiring the driver to maintain references to options in a deeply coupled way. Moving this logic into the {{AutoEncrypter}} will further decouple and simplify the driver CSFLE implementation.

summary: Add "connectionError" as a valid "reason" for a ConnectionCheckOutFailedEvent when connection set up fails
description: See DRIVERS-688 for more information.

summary: Integrate CMAP connection pool into unified topology
description: Now that we have a new connection pool it should replace the use of the legacy pool in the unified {{Topology}} type.

summary: Server descriptions with me mismatch from primary response should be removed
description: Due to a missing branch in the pseudocode for {{updateRsFromPrimary}} in the SDAM specification, server responses with a me mismatch from a primary responder are not removed from the working set.

summary: BSON Double and Int32 types do not handle Number Objects gracefully
client platform not sent in metadata for CMAP connections
description: The new CMAP pool fails to send the correct platform in the client metadata during connection handshake.

summary: Improve traceability of CMAP events
description: Val did a basic evaluation of the new CMAP events and has requested that we include things like {{connectionId}} and {{duration}} in events where that would make sense. It would also greatly improve users ability to trace across monitoring if there was a standardized way to reference a particular connection on a particular connection pool.

summary: "readPreference.equals is not a function" exception thrown within executeWithServerSelection
Replset events no longer firing after upgrade from 3.1.15 to 3.4.1
Use a queue for server selection
description: The server selection loop is currently implemented as a rather complicated system of timers, calls to `selectServers` and listening for specific description changed events. This could all be replaced with something similar to the new connection pool's wait queue, greatly decreasing the complexity of this piece of code.

summary: db.findOne does not respect client setting bufferMaxEntries
description:
Steps to Reproduce: 
1. Connect to a single server, with bufferMaxEntries=0
2. Stop the server
3. Query the server using db.collection().findOne, promise not resolve
4. Query the server using db.colllection().aggregate, promise got error, "MongoError:no connection available for operation and number of stored operation > 0"
5. Start the server
6. Query in step 3 above (findOne) got fires, and the promise is resolved with result

Is this the intended behaviour?

summary: CMAP connection pool fails to create connection using SSL / TLS
description:
SSL certificate & key are removed from pool.options, as they're not listed under VALID_POOL_OPTIONS. When connection pool calls createConnection(pool), it fails as certificate and key are missing.

Furthermore, resulting error is silently ignored because pool._propagateError(err) doesn't do anything.

summary: retryWrites seems not to work as expected in failover test
Server v4.2.2 segfaults when calling mapReduce with a `query` that contains $ne
Unified topology does not transition server to Unknown on non-timeout operation error
description: The driver currently ignores all non-"state change" errors for the purposes of SDAM. Instead, it should transition a server to {{Unknown}} if non-timeout network errors are encountered, as dictated by the error handling section of the SDAM spec.

summary: Add exhaust command support to transport layer
description: Now that we support the {{exhaustAllowed}} bit for commands which support exhaust behavior, changes are required at the transport level to support the feature. Specifically, {{command}} needs to support an {{exhaustAllowed}} option, and the {{Connection}} needs to be able to track {{responseTo}}/{{requestId}} of synthetic {{getMore}}s and use the same callback for new batches.

summary: Multiple incoming message packets are not processed correctly
description: The new CMAP {{Connection}} type does not handle multiple inband messages properly, and will call the stream callback multiple times. It also does not properly call the callback when there is not enough data received for a full message

summary: Expand use of error labels for RetryableWrites
description: See DRIVERS-525 for details.

summary: Make ExceededTimeLimit retryable writes error
description: See DRIVERS-651 for details.

summary: Simplify server error handling in unified topology
description: The {{Topology}} type currently is deeply coupled with error handling at the {{Server}} level. When an error occurs, the {{Server}} emits an {{error}} event, which in turn is processed by the {{Topology}} which calls {{emit}} back on the {{Server}} instance - a bit of a rats nest. Since there is nothing about error handling that needs details in the {{Topology}}, we can move all of this code into {{Server}} directly.

summary: Deprecate oplogReplay find command option from CRUD spec
description: See DRIVERS-710 for details.

summary: Cursor.hasNext() seems to consume first document when the cursor is piped in a writable stream
Do not run minimum connection size task if there is no minimum pool size
description: The modern CMAP connection pool runs a background "thread" to maintain a minimum connection pool size, however, the default minimum pool size is 0. We should not run this "thread" when there is no minimum pool size.

summary: max election ID comparison uses invalid comparison for tracking
description: The logic in the unified topology for max election ID tracking assumes that the values being compared are in Extended JSON, because the test runner does not convert values to BSON for us. The logic should be updated to actually compare {{ObjectId}} instances.

summary: MongoServerSelectionError when using useUnifiedTopology: true
description:
I appear to be getting issues when reading from secondary members when the useUnifiedTopology flag is set to true.

 _Our use case is to read some collections from secondary members to reduce strain on the primary, such as calculating stats, while other are reading from the primary_

The error is the following

 
{noformat}
err { MongoServerSelectionError: Server selection timed out after 30000 ms
    at Timeout.waitQueueMember.timer.setTimeout [as _onTimeout] (/Users/paulrobinson/workspace/akero/investigate/mongoose/native_secondary/node_modules/mongodb/lib/core/sdam/topology.js:428:30)
    at ontimeout (timers.js:436:11)
    at tryOnTimeout (timers.js:300:5)
    at listOnTimeout (timers.js:263:5)
    at Timer.processTimers (timers.js:223:10)
  name: 'MongoServerSelectionError',
  reason:
   TopologyDescription {
     type: 'ReplicaSetWithPrimary',
     setName: 'rs0',
     maxSetVersion: 3,
     maxElectionId: null,
     servers:
      Map {
        'localhost:27017' => [ServerDescription],
        'localhost:27018' => [ServerDescription],
        'localhost:27016' => [ServerDescription] },
     stale: false,
     compatible: true,
     compatibilityError: null,
     logicalSessionTimeoutMinutes: 30,
     heartbeatFrequencyMS: 10000,
     localThresholdMS: 15,
     commonWireVersion: 6 },
  [Symbol(mongoErrorContextSymbol)]: {} }{noformat}
 

Here is the code and outputs

[https://gist.github.com/Niffy/d07e33e1301dd793996ba28df8b0182b]

_Sorry I did not paste here, it's medium-ish in size._

The general idea of the code is to try and read from a collection, but from the secondaries only. It attempts to read from the collection 20 times at the same time to help illustrate the bug.

Majority of the time when useUnifiedTopology is set to true, the code will hang and throw the error.  Sometimes it will work just fine, but if you step down the primary and try again, you get the same error thrown. 
 * output-1-true.txt - You can see here it never got any results whatsoever
 * output-2-true.txt - You get some results then the error
 * output-3-false.txt - You get no errors when the useUnifiedTopology flag is set to false. This is also fine after doing a step down on the primary.

 

 

 The only way to avoid this is to set useUnifiedTopology to false.
 * mongodb driver 3.5.3

 * mongodb replica set running on 3.6.15 and 4.2.0

summary: Driver does not seem to properly recover from a long disconnection with primary server
description:
Tested driver version: 3.5.3

Steps to reproduce:
 * You'll need two machines (on the same network) to reproduce this; one called {{client}}, one called {{server}}. toxiproxy needs to be installed on {{client}}.
 * On {{server}}, run a replicat set with 3 nodes (port 8500, 8501, 8502). Ensure that the node listening on 8500 is the primary one.
 * On {{client}}, run toxiproxy to open three local ports, and redirect traffic to {{server}} (in this example, 10.10.164.148 is the IP address of {{server}}):
{code}toxiproxy-cli create mongo_proxied_0 -l 127.0.0.1:8500 -u 10.10.164.148:8500 
toxiproxy-cli create mongo_proxied_1 -l 127.0.0.1:8501 -u 10.10.164.148:8501
toxiproxy-cli create mongo_proxied_2 -l 127.0.0.1:8502 -u 10.10.164.148:8502
{code}
 * On {{client}}, edit /etc/hosts to make sure that any request sent with {{server}}'s hostname are actually sent to localhost (otherwise the application running on {{client}} will connect directly to {{server}}, instead of going through toxiproxy)
 * On {{client}}, run the following nodejs script:
{code:javascript}const mongodb = require("mongodb");
const MongoClient = mongodb.MongoClient;
const net = require("net");


async function wait(t) {
	return new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, t));
}

function log() {
	console.log.apply(console, [new Date(), ...arguments]);
}
const URL = "mongodb://127.0.0.1:8500,127.0.0.1:8501,127.0.0.1:8502/testDb?readPreference=primaryPreferred&slaveOk=true&replicaSet=rs0&heartbeatFrequencyMS=10000&serverSelectionTimeoutMS=30000";
let client;
async function getClient() {
	if (!client) {
		log("mongo - Connecting to " + URL);
		client = await MongoClient.connect(URL, { useUnifiedTopology: true });
		log("mongo - Connected");
	}

	return client;
}

async function loop() {
	while(true) {
		try {
			log("mongo - Connecting");
			const client = await getClient();
			const db = client.db();
			const collection = db.collection("testCollection");
			await collection.insertOne({name: Date.now()});
			log("mongo - Write done");
			await wait(1000);
			log("mongo - Done waiting");
		} catch (err) {
			log("mongo - Error:", err);
		}
	}
}

function loop_connect() {
	const client = new net.Socket();
	const port = 8500;
	log("network - connecting to 127.0.0.1 on port", port);
	client.on("error", (err) => {
		log("network - Failed:", err.message);
		client.destroy();
	});
	client.connect(port, "127.0.0.1", () => {
		log("network - Connected!");
		client.destroy();
	});
}

loop();
setInterval(loop_connect, 1000);
{code}
 ** Every second, this script will try to insert a document. The resulting logs are prefixed with "mongo"
 ** In parallel, every second, this script will try to open a socket to 127.0.0.1:8500. The resulting logs are prefixed with "network"
 * On {{client}}, assuming toxiproxy server runs on port 8474, run the following script to cut the connexion between our nodejs client and the primary node during 21 seconds:
{code}curl http://localhost:8474/proxies/mongo_proxied_0 -d '{"enabled":false}'
sleep 21
curl http://localhost:8474/proxies/mongo_proxied_0 -d '{"enabled":true}'
{code}

What happens:
 * Thanks to the "network" logs (see some logs I captured below), we can see that our nodejs client is indeed unable to connect to localhost:8500 during 21 seconds (from 14:12:39 to 14:13:00)
 * In "mongo" logs, we can see that:
 ** 30 seconds after the disconnection, at 14:13:09, the following error is logged: Error: MongoServerSelectionError: connect ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:8500. I guess this is due to the fact that the previous heartbeat failed; this seems okay to me.
 ** However, right after, we try to send another request to mongo (at 14:13:09), and it fails 30 seconds later (at 14:13:39) with the following error message: Error: MongoServerSelectionError: Server selection timed out after 30000 ms. This seems like a bug to me: the primary node was up and reachable during all that time, as confirmed by the "network" logs. But the driver was unable to reconnect and execute the request during this timeframe.

{code}2020-02-24T14:12:36.862Z mongo - Connecting

{code}

summary: topology state should transition to closing before draining the wait queue
description: Since the {{Topology}} transitions the internal state to {{CLOSING}} _after_ the wait queue is drained, it can lead to spurious errors at client close due to conflicting states

summary: remove core module completely
description: Now that we no longer have a core and native driver, keeping everything in the {{lib/core}} folder is needless fragmentation of the codebase. Files inside {{lib/core}} should be moved to more logical locations.

summary: Stop emitting topology events from `Db`
Investigate double close events in Unified Topology
description:
The test:

{{functional/operation_example/shouldCorrectlyOpenASimpleDbSingleServerConnection}} 

fails when the {{done}} callback is called from a {{close}} event handler rather than being passed as a callback i.e. {{client.close(done)}}, as {{done}} gets called twice.

This only occurs when {{MONGODB_UNIFIED_TOPOLOGY=1}}.

summary: Remove callback support for all methods that return a cursor
Upgrade and Add BSON dependency to mongodb-mock-server
description: The mongodb-mock-server does not list a BSON dependency so it operates off off the drivers dependency. Add BSON as its own dependency and fix issues caused by 1.x -> 4.x changes.

summary: Mock server still has BSON as property
description: Make sure mock-server uses BSON the dependency and not a property passed in.

summary: Use nyc for test coverage
description: https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/pull/2257

summary: Update MongoClient documentation include unified topology documentation
description: The next major version of the driver will remove legacy topologies and replace them with the unified topology. This means that many options to {{MongoClient}} will no longer be relevant, and new ones have been introduced.

summary: Upgrade driver to BSON 4.x
description: Upgrade the Node Driver to use BSON v4 internally.

summary: BSON serialization ignores unknown _bsontype
BSON loses type information when automatically upgrading symbols to strings
description: BSON v4 automatically upgrades {{BSONSymbol}} types to a {{String}} when deserializing, losing type information. This means we are unable to properly round trip these values, and currently breaks CSFLE tests.

summary: watch API changeStream not notified of closed connection
